name: NOAA CO-OPS Vocabulary
description: Domain terms and definitions for the NOAA Center for Operational Oceanographic Products and Services (CO-OPS) APIs covering tides, currents, water levels, and oceanographic data.

terms.label: Water Level
terms.definition: The observed height of the water surface above or below a reference datum, measured by a tide gauge. Includes storm surge, tides, and long-term sea level change.

terms.label: Tide Predictions
terms.definition: Astronomical tide predictions computed from harmonic constituents, representing the expected water level due to gravitational effects of the moon and sun only (excluding weather effects).

terms.label: High/Low Water
terms.definition: The predicted or observed times and heights of high and low tide events within a day.

terms.label: Hourly Height
terms.definition: Verified hourly water level heights, averaged from 6-minute observations.

terms.label: Daily Mean
terms.definition: Daily mean water level computed from hourly observations.

terms.label: Wind
terms.definition: Sustained wind speed and direction at the station, averaged over an interval.

terms.label: Air Pressure
terms.definition: Barometric (atmospheric) pressure at the station.

terms.label: Air Temperature
terms.definition: Air temperature measured at the station.

terms.label: Water Temperature
terms.definition: Temperature of the water surface at the station sensor.

terms.label: Conductivity
terms.definition: Electrical conductivity of water at the station, which is related to salinity.

terms.label: Salinity
terms.definition: Salt concentration of the water at the station, derived from conductivity measurements.

terms.label: Visibility
terms.definition: Horizontal visibility measured at the station, typically by a forward scatter sensor.

terms.label: Relative Humidity
terms.definition: Relative humidity of the air at the station as a percentage.

terms.label: Currents
terms.definition: Observed water current velocity and direction from acoustic Doppler current profiler (ADCP) or acoustic current meter (ACM) sensors.

terms.label: Currents Predictions
terms.definition: Predicted tidal current velocity and direction computed from harmonic constituents.

terms.label: Air Gap
terms.definition: The vertical clearance between the water surface and the underside of a bridge or structure, measured at a specific location.

terms.label: Mean Lower Low Water
terms.definition: The average of the lower of the two daily low waters observed over the National Tidal Datum Epoch (1983-2001). The standard tidal datum used for most NOAA tide data.

terms.label: Mean Higher High Water
terms.definition: The average of the higher of the two daily high waters over the National Tidal Datum Epoch.

terms.label: Mean High Water
terms.definition: The average of all high water heights over the National Tidal Datum Epoch.

terms.label: Mean Low Water
terms.definition: The average of all low water heights over the National Tidal Datum Epoch.

terms.label: Mean Sea Level
terms.definition: The arithmetic mean of hourly water levels over the National Tidal Datum Epoch.

terms.label: Mean Tide Level
terms.definition: The arithmetic mean of mean high water and mean low water.

terms.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
terms.definition: A fixed geodetic vertical datum established by the National Geodetic Survey. Used as reference for most modern US elevation data.

terms.label: Station Datum
terms.definition: The arbitrary fixed reference datum unique to each tide station, used as the zero point for all gauge measurements.

terms.label: International Great Lakes Datum
terms.definition: Vertical datum used for Great Lakes water level measurements.

terms.label: Harmonic Constituents
terms.definition: Amplitude and phase values for tidal harmonic components (e.g., M2, S2, K1, O1) derived from observed water levels. Used to predict future tides.

terms.label: Tidal Datums
terms.definition: Computed statistical water levels (MLLW, MHW, MSL, etc.) used as reference planes for water level measurements at a station.

terms.label: National Water Level Observation Network
terms.definition: NOAA's primary network of long-term, continuously operating tide stations that form the backbone of the National Tidal Datum Epoch.

terms.label: Physical Oceanographic Real-Time System
terms.definition: NOAA's real-time environmental monitoring systems deployed in major US ports and harbors to support safe and efficient maritime navigation.

terms.label: High Tide Flooding
terms.definition: Also called 'nuisance flooding' or 'sunny day flooding', occurs when water levels exceed minor flood thresholds during high tide events without storm activity.

terms.label: Sea Level Trend
terms.definition: The long-term rate of change in mean sea level at a station, expressed in millimeters per year, calculated from monthly mean sea level data.

terms.label: Storm Surge
terms.definition: The abnormal rise in sea level during a storm event above predicted tidal levels, caused by strong winds and low atmospheric pressure.
